data.title: United Spirits shares fall as Macquarie maintains 'underperform'
data.description:
United Spirits shares have been on a decent run, rallying almost 40 percent since the start of the year.

Shares of United Spirits Limited were off to a weak start on the bourses on October 18 after it fell over a percent to Rs 1,507 after Macquarie maintained its 'underperform' recommendation on the counter citing stiff competition may likely hamper growth prospects.

With a price target of Rs 1,100, the international brokerage forecasts a downside potential of 28 percent from the last closing price of Rs 1,528. United Spirits shares have been on a decent run, rallying almost 40 percent since the start of the year.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Follow our LIVE blog for all the latest market updates

The international brokerage suggests that Pernod India, a key competitor, has indicated that it is outpacing the industry, reporting 2 percent sales growth in the September 2024 quarter. Pernod expects this sales momentum to fully reverse in the quarter ending December 2024, with strong growth anticipated for the fiscal year ending June 2025.

India has become the largest market by volume and the second-largest by value for French spirits giant Pernod Ricard, known for brands like Absolut, Chivas Regal, and Glenlivet. Pernod Ricard's Indian portfolio also includes well-known IMFL brands such as Royal Stag, Blenders Pride, and Imperial Blue. India now accounts for 12 percent of Pernod Ricard’s global revenue, with the U.S. remaining the company's top market by value.

As per reports, Pernod Ricard India (PRI) reported consolidated revenue of Rs 25,039.47 crore for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2023. Although not publicly traded, the company is second only to United Spirits, owned by Diageo, in the Indian market.

In the first quarter of FY25, United Spirit's net profit grew around 2 percent year over year to Rs 485 crore, up from Rs 477 crore. Revenue also rose 3.5 percent to Rs 2,761 crore, up from Rs 2,667 crore in the corresponding quarter of the preceding fiscal year.

Even though the topline and bottom line growth seems modest, it was still above what most brokerages anticipated. The company's growth was anticipated to be weak on year due to inflation's impact on price-sensitive consumers.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

At about 9:20 am, shares of the company were trading at Rs 1,510, lower by 1.3 percent from the last close on the NSE.

data.title: Axis Bank's robust Q2 prompts bullish calls from brokerages; stock jumps 4%
data.description:
The lender's net interest income rose nearly 10 percent year-on-year to Rs 13,533 crore

Shares of Axis Bank were off to a strong start on the bourses on October 18 after rising 4 percent to Rs 1,172 as it delivered an impressive second quarter with net profit surging to Rs 6,917.57 crore, driven by robust core lending income amid strong credit demand. Following this performance, several brokerages have issued bullish calls on the stock.

The lender's net interest income rose nearly 10 percent year-on-year to Rs 13,533 crore, up from Rs 12,315 crore in the previous year. The total income increased to Rs 37,142 crore in the reporting period from Rs 31,660 crore crore a year ago. Sequentially, the lender reported Rs 35,844 crore in the June 2024 quarter.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Follow our LIVE blog for all the latest market updates

What should you do with the stock? Here's what experts have to say. 

Morgan Stanley maintains an 'Overweight' call on Axis Bank with a target price of Rs 1,445 per share. The bank’s asset quality showed improvement in Q2, easing concerns that arose after the Q1 results. Credit costs were much lower on a quarter-on-quarter basis, and one-off gains were used to bolster contingency provisions. While core revenue growth remained subdued, it is expected to pick up as deposit growth improves over the next year, analysts added.

Nomura has issued a 'Buy' call suggesting that the quarter was steady, aligning with muted expectations. Loan and deposit growth were soft on a quarterly basis but in line with expectations. Higher write-offs and lower net slippages contributed to improved gross non-performing loans (NPL). Treasury gains helped offset weaker core Pre-Provision Operating Profit (PPOP), and one-off tax gains were used to strengthen provision buffers.

Macquarie continues to hold its 'Outperform' with a target price of Rs 1,400. The bank had a decent Q2, though growth concerns linger. Tax write-backs and treasury gains helped drive a positive surprise in profit after tax (PAT). While the balance sheet was strengthened by contingent buffers, there is still a focus on managing incremental stress.

Investec with a 'Buy' rating, says that while near-term growth may remain subdued, the bank’s management continues to hold a positive long-term outlook. Asset quality performed better than expected during the quarter.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad
Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Bernstein has an 'Outperform' rating on Axis Bank. The international brokerage added that even as the bank posted a profit beat, asset quality concerns persisted, and loan growth was weak. However, operational expenses have slowed, and non-interest income (NOI) was used to push the bank’s return on assets (RoA) above 1.8 percent.

In the July-September quarter, Axis Bank's total deposit grew by 14 percent on-year to Rs 10.87 lakh crore, from Rs 9.56 lakh crore in a year ago period. The current account and savings account (CASA) deposit of the bank grew to Rs 4.41 lakh crore in the reporting quarter, from Rs 4.24 lakh crore in a year ago period.

data.title: Wipro rolls out salary hikes for FY25 effective from Sept 1
data.description:
Wipro's operating margin expanded despite absorbing one month of salary increase, rolled out in September.

Wipro announced a pay hike from September 1, Chief Executive Officer Srinivas Pallia said while addressing the media after declaring results for the first quarter ended September 30, 2024. Even after the hikes, the company was able to increase its operating margin in the September quarter.

“We implemented merit salary increase effective September 1, just nine months after the last cycle,” Pallia said.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Most information technology (IT) companies have deferred salary increments to the third quarter to maintain their operating margins amid a tepid demand environment. While TCS rolled out hikes in Q2, HCLTech said it will implement a 7 percent salary hike from October, with top performers set to receive raises of up to 12-15 percent.

Infosys, on the other hand, announced that it will implement salary hikes in two phases, with junior employees receiving increments in January and the rest in April.

Wipro Chief Financial Officer Aparna Iyer added that a unique business cycle in 2023 caused delays in salary increments, but the September rollout will establish a normal hike cycle going forward. In 2023, IT companies deferred salary hikes as macroeconomic uncertainties led to a tepid demand environment.

Iyer added that the company’s operating margin for the quarter stood at 16.8 percent, which is a sequential increase of 35 basis points. “This expansion in margins was achieved through operational improvements, even after absorbing one month of salary increases for our associates rolled out in September,” she said.

Fresher Hiring, Backlogs

Saurabh Govil, Wipro's chief human resources officer, provided updates on fresher hiring. He said that the company has been consistently onboarding between 2,500 and 3,000 freshers every quarter since Q1 of this fiscal year.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

In July, Wipro said it plans to hire 10,000 to 12,000 people, including on and off campus, for the financial year 2025.

When asked about fresher backlog, he assured that it would be cleared by the end of Q3.

Several IT companies, including Wipro, delayed the onboarding of freshers in 2022 and 2023 following a revenue decline caused by reduced discretionary spending in their key markets, the US and Europe, amid macroeconomic uncertainties.

The Bengaluru-based company added 978 employees in Q2, signalling that the company is pacing itself for growth. This comes at a time when Wipro is reassessing its lateral and campus hiring models to focus on improving utilisation rate of existing employees amidst lowering attrition, to grow margins.

Wipro corrected its Q1 headcount on October 17, attributing the discrepancy to an error from one of its entities. Total headcount for the quarter stood at 233,889, which is 10,818 fewer employees year-on-year from Q2FY24 when the headcount was 244,707.

data.title: Retail investors betting big on PSUs even as shares dip from record highs
data.description:
Among the stocks with the highest surge in retail investors, Canara Bank led with 4.26 lakh new retail investors, followed by IREDA, Bank of Baroda, and HUDCO, which added 3.7 lakh, 2.86 lakh, and 2.83 lakh investors, respectively

The September quarter saw the number of retail shareholders increasing in most PSUs even as shares of majority of government-owned entities declined. Data shows that out of 68 PSU companies that have already reported their September quarter shareholding pattern, as many as 56 saw a rise in retail investor base.

However, out of this only 16 of these entities delivered positive returns during the quarter, while the rest saw their shares decline. PSUs like Canara Bank, IREDA, Bank of Baroda and HUDCO among others saw the biggest jump in the number of retail shareholders in the September quarter even as they have dropped in the range of 20-30 percent from their respective 52-weeks highs.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Among the stocks with the highest surge in retail investors, Canara Bank led with 4.26 lakh new retail investors, followed by IREDA, Bank of Baroda, and HUDCO, which added 3.7 lakh, 2.86 lakh, and 2.83 lakh investors, respectively.

Notably, all these stocks have declined from their 52-week highs. Canara Bank dropped over 19 percent from its peak in June 2024, IREDA fell by 29 percent, and Bank of Baroda declined by 18 percent.

Other PSU stocks like MTNL, SJVN, Bharat Electronics, Coal India, Rail Vikas Nigam, Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders, Ircon International, and State Bank of India also saw a rise in new retail investors, with increases of 2.73 lakh, 2.55 lakh, 2.51 lakh, 2.32 lakh, 2.21 lakh, 1.92 lakh, 1.85 lakh, and 1.55 lakh, respectively.

However, MTNL and SJVN lost over 37 percent and 49 percent, respectively while Bharat Electronics and Coal India declined by 16 percent and 8 percent, respectively. Rail Vikas Nigam and Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders fell by over 26 percent, and Ircon International and SBI dropped 36 percent and 12 percent, respectively.

Interestingly, analysts believe that retail investors are falling into a trap as over the past year, a common strategy has been to buy on dips, with the assumption that any 2-3 percent decline in PSU stocks presents a buying opportunity.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad
Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

This approach has gained traction among retail investors who entered the market in the last three to four years, as they've seen PSU stocks bounce back repeatedly, add analysts highlighting that many retail investors keep averaging down on every drop, treating each decline like a discounted sale.

However, since the beginning of July, PSU stocks have entered a correction phase. Analysts attribute this to the fact that PSU stocks have become overvalued after a significant rally over the past two to three years.

Sectors like defence and railways have seen substantial gains, with defence stocks, for instance, surging by as much as 15 times, while their earnings have only grown about four times on average. This mismatch suggests that stock prices are outpacing profitability, creating inflated price-to-earnings (PE) ratios that are not justified by underlying fundamentals.

Ambareesh Baliga, independent market analyst cautions retail investors to be more discerning about the stocks they purchase and the valuations they accept. Blindly buying or averaging down just because a stock has fallen does not necessarily make it a bargain, he says while adding that strategies that worked well during a bull market might not hold up in a corrective market environment.

Analysts believe that PSU stocks might face further corrections in the short term as valuations normalise. Like a pendulum, the market tends to swing between undervaluation and overvaluation, and a correction could push these stocks closer to fair value, or even below it, they say.

Meanwhile, retail investor participation has surged in other PSU stocks as well like Garden Reach Shipbuilders & Engineers, Shipping Corp of India, BHEL, IFCI, Bharat Dynamics, RITES, Railtel Corp of India, and LIC Housing Finance.

In some cases, however, investors have become cautious. For instance, as LIC's stock dropped 23 percent in the September quarter, the number of retail shareholders fell by 1.6 lakh. Similar trends were seen in NALCO and BPCL, as stock prices declined by around 7 percent each and the number of retail investors dipped by 42,000 and 21,000, respectively. Other stocks like HPCL, IOB, Central Bank of India, and Bank of India, which saw declines of 20-35 percent, also experienced a drop in retail shareholding.

data.title: The indefatigable optimism of the Indian consumer
data.description:
-

One of the defining traits of the RBI’s consumer confidence surveys has been the contrast between the Indian consumers’ current dismal state and her unrelenting optimism about the future. For example, the survey’s Current Situation Index (CSI) has remained below the 100 mark, which indicates pessimism, since December 2016 almost continuously, except for one or two exceptions. data.title: Brokerages cheer as Infosys raises guidance for yet another quarter
data.description:
ADRs of Infosys fell over 1 percent overnight on the New York Stock Exchange after the release of it Q2 results.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

IT giant Infosys delivered positive Q2 earnings, but the highlight was the company's guidance lift, for the second quarter in a row. Buoyed by this, brokerages also remain optimistic about the upward revision, which hints at early signs of a recovery in discretionary spending.

Infosys raised its FY25 revenue guidance to 3.75-4.5 percent in constant currency terms, an increase from the previous quarter’s range of 3-4 percent. Infosys' revenue guidance lift was driven by improved and broad-based Q2 performance, increased volumes, and growth in its small-deal pipeline.  The company's operating margin guidance for FY25 remained steady at 20-22 percent.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Nuvama Institutional Equities forecasts Infosys to benefit disproportionately in FY26-27 from a rebound in discretionary spending, much like it was disproportionately impacted in FY24-25 compared to peers. On that account, Nuvama sees Infosys as one of the best plays for capturing the IT sector's revival in the coming years.

Nomura also shares the view and believes Infosys to be one of the best ways to play a potential improvement in discretionary demand in IT services, reiterating the stock as its top pick within the sector. Nomura has a 'buy' call on the stock with a price target of Rs 2,130.

Follow our market blog to catch all the live action

Despite the guidance upgrade, Infosys posted a slight miss in its Q2 earnings. The company's consolidated net profit stood at Rs 6,506 crore for Q2FY25, a 2.2 percent sequential increase but below Moneycontrol's estimate of Rs 6,769 crore. Revenue from operations rose 4.2 percent on quarter to Rs 40,986 crore, narrowly missing the forecast of Rs 40,857 crore.

On the back this slight miss in Q2, Morgan Stanley sees the possibility of a bit of stock correction in the near-term. However, the brokerage also feels that investors should build fresh positions in such a potential correction. Retaining a bullish stance on Infosys, Morgan Stanley has an 'overweight' call on the stock with a target price of Rs 2,150.

On the technical level, Morgan Stanley sees a strong support coming in for the stock at its five-year average free-cash-flow multiple--which sits at Rs 1,780.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Meanwhile, the IT major's deal wins stood at $2.4 billion in Q2 lowest in the last five quarters, which Emkay Institutional Equities attributes primarily to the lumpy nature of the deals. However, the brokerage highlighted that deal pipeline for Infosys still remains strong with small-deal pipeline seeing double-digit sequential growth. Emkay also holds a 'buy' call on Infosys with a target price of Rs 2,150.

Nomura also believes expanding pipeline of small deals augurs well for Infosys' discretionary demand.

Also Read | Infosys ADR falls nearly 4% on NYSE following Q2 results

data.title: Brokerages cut target price on Havells India following Q2 margin miss
data.description:
Contribution margins for Havells (ex-Lloyd) declined 200 bps on-year to 20.7 percent.

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Havells India's earnings report for the July-September quarter 2024 failed to enthuse brokerages as margins failed to meet expectations.

The consumer durables player reported 9.5 percent on-year increase in net profit at Rs 273 crore for the quarter ended September 30, 2024 as compared to Rs 249 crore in the year-ago period.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The company's revenue from operations rose 16 percent to Rs 4,533 crore in Q2FY25 as against Rs 3,891 crore in Q2FY24.

The revenue growth was led by a robust showing in cables & wires and electrical consumer durables segments. "However, margins suffered from commodity price volatility and ahead-of-schedule A&P spending," said domestic brokerage Nuvama Institutional Equities.

Contribution margins for Havells (ex-Lloyd) declined 200 bps on-year to 20.7 percent while EBIT margin dipped 300 bps YoY to 9.9 percent due to higher advertising and promotional spends.

Follow our market blog to catch all the live updates

Japanese brokerage Nomura maintained its 'neutral' call on the FMEG player, stating that the current valuations are factoring in a recovery. Excluding Lloyd, Nomura believes that Havells India can maintain the 16 percent revenue growth momentum until FY27.

The growth will come from the premiumisation push in ECD and new capacity building in the W&C segment. However, delayed price hikes and slow demand can delay margin improvements.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Jefferies noted that the miss in the margins offsets all healthy sales growth. The brokerage cut its target price to Rs 1,870 from Rs 1,880 earlier. However, it noted that the offtake for the festive season has started on a positive note.

Investec also slashed its target price to Rs 1,850 from Rs 1,910 on the margin miss. The brokerage is pencilling in lower margin expectations for FY25-27, leading to a 3-5 percent cut in EPS estimates.

data.title: Morgan Stanley 'Underweight' on Tata Chemicals stock after Q2 profit plunges 55%
data.description:
In the past 12 months, Tata Chemicals stock has risen 3 percent. In comparison, Nifty rose 25 percent during this period.

Shares of Tata Chemicals may witness some pressure on October 18 after Morgan Stanley maintained an 'Underweight' rating on the stock with a target price of Rs 880 per share following the company's disappointing earnings report for the quarter ended September 2024.

Tata Chemicals reported a near 55 percent on-year fall in its second-quarter profit, hurt by lower soda ash prices and higher freight costs. The chemical maker's consolidated net profit fell to Rs 194 crore, down from Rs 428 crore a year ago.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Its revenue from operations rose 0.03 percent YoY to Rs 3,999 crore while total expenses rose nearly 7 percent YoY to Rs 3,803 crore, with freight and forwarding charges rising nearly 32 percent.

Tata Chemicals' earnings were broadly in line with estimates, although its India margins were impacted by adverse weather conditions. In contrast, performance in the US segment met expectations, while Kenya delivered a positive surprise.

Despite this, Basic Chemistry EBITDA per tonne declined by 10 percent quarter-on-quarter (QoQ). Morgan Stanley highlighted that the ramp-up of Chinese capacity and the limited evidence of supply rationalization pose challenges for a near-term margin recovery.

Follow our market blog to catch all the live action

An economic downturn in China and sluggish demand in the EU have significantly impacted soda ash prices, Tata Chemicals' primary business segment. According to analysts at Nirmal Bang, global soda ash prices dropped by nearly 23 percent during the July-September quarter. Tata Chemicals had already reduced soda ash prices last November.

Analysts have also warned that chemical companies may face rising costs due to increased container and freight charges amid the Middle East crisis.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Tata Chemicals shares ended 2.3 percent lower at Rs 1,074.90 on National Stock Exchange (NSE) in the previous session. The stock has fallen around 4 percent so far this year, underperforming Nifty's returns of 14 percent.

In the past 12 months, the counter has risen 3 percent. In comparison, Nifty rose 25 percent during this period.
